notes: Pinned OCI SDK contract: ListSubscriptions is the only top-level Subscription operation in v65.110.0. The request requires compartmentId plus exactly one of planNumber, subscriptionId, or buyerEmail, optionally accepts isCommitInfoRequired, and returns Items []SubscriptionSummary.

Pinned provider coverage is unconfirmed. formal/sources.lock still points at github.com/oracle/terraform-provider-oci@eb653febb1bab4cc6650a96d404a8baf36fdf671, but the accessible provider inventory did not expose a matching OneSubscription Subscription resource or data source. The providerResource and datasource names therefore stay provisional OCI-style placeholders so generator-owned registry artifacts can derive stable links without claiming verified provider coverage.

Repo-authored runtime contract is narrower than the visible SDK surface: the published kind is query-backed and observe-only, paginates ListSubscriptions for every reconcile, requires exactly one returned summary, publishes no create/update/delete/get path, and intentionally leaves status.status.ocid empty because the top-level SubscriptionSummary does not expose a truthful tracked identity.

The top-level summary status is an untyped business-state string rather than a typed OCI lifecycle enum. The runtime requeues only clearly transitional create-like and update-like tokens, treats FAIL/ERROR values as terminal failure, and otherwise settles success while keeping the raw sdkStatus visible on the CR. Delete is Kubernetes-local cleanup only because the SDK exposes no delete helper for the top-level summary.
